projectDescription: A personal dashboard for Universidad Nacional de Trujillo (UNT) students that automates SUV portal access via headless browser scraping, extracts all academic data in a single session, and presents it through a clean Next.js interface.
keyFeatures: Automated SUV login with OCR-based CAPTCHA solving (up to 3 retries)
Real-time grade display per course with unit breakdown (U1–U6) and inhibition flag
Grade predictor that enumerates all valid note combinations to achieve ≥14 average
Weekly schedule grid derived from attendance records
Schedule optimizer finding conflict-free section combinations with best score
Attendance tracking with risk alerts per course
Full academic history across all periods with weighted averages
Period-by-period analytics with pass rates and course retry detection
No credential persistence — credentials exist only during the active WebSocket session
Docker Compose and Railway deployment ready
publicApiSurface: WebSocket /ws — single endpoint for all client–server communication
WS incoming: { username: string, password: string }
WS event: dashboard_ready — full DashboardReport payload
WS event: error — { message: string, category: string }
DashboardReport.grade_report — grades per course with unit scores and prediction
DashboardReport.student_profile — personal and academic profile data
DashboardReport.academic_record — full course history across all periods
DashboardReport.attendance — per-course attendance percentage and risk flag
connectAndFetch(username, password, callbacks) — frontend WebSocket client
